reason: Codex (gpt-5.4-codex, xhigh) verified the ModernTCN classification reimplementation against the official luodhhh/ModernTCN classification reference: branch summation, BatchNorm-fusion math, block ordering (DWConv->BN->ConvFFN1 groups=nvars->ConvFFN2 groups=dmodel->residual) and the ConvFFN2 permute/reshape path, stem/downsample replicate-padding, head_nf sizing, and the flatten+GELU+dropout+Linear head all match the reference; shape probes pass for Ethanol (->C=4), FaceDetection (->C=2), Handwriting (->C=26). One real bug found: ReparamLargeKernelConv.merge_kernel() deleted the large/small branches but forward() still referenced them, breaking the optional inference path. Applied Codex's minimal fix (forward dispatches to lkb_reparam when merged) to both methods/moderntcn answer.md and reasoning.md; re-verified post-merge forward matches the two-branch output to ~1e-7. The trajectory finale answer (04-moderntcn-answer.md) keeps only the train-time two-branch form (no merge API) and is faithful. Codex itself could not write files (read-only sandbox); the fix and this verdict were applied by the author from Codex's findings.
